current.summary: Capability monitor pipeline design — identified new provider bootstrap as architecturally distinct from change detection

activeWork.description: Designing capability monitor pipeline: change detection vs new provider bootstrap flows
activeWork.progress: Identified architectural gap: new provider bootstrap (no cached hash, no format doc, no seeder spec baseline) is distinct from change detection flow. Change detection assumes baseline exists; bootstrap starts from zero. Design doc needs new Bootstrap Flow section with separate state machine and transitions.

recentDecisions: Bootstrap flow is separate from change detection — bootstrap has no baseline to compare against
New provider case requires distinct architectural handling with its own state transitions
Bootstrap path: fetch format doc → generate seeder spec → extract capabilities → cache
nextSteps: Add Bootstrap Flow section to capability monitor design doc
Define state transitions for bootstrap path (separate from change detection flow)
Document provider state lifecycle: no-baseline (bootstrap) vs has-baseline (change detection)

context.notes: Capability monitor needs two distinct architectural flows: (1) Bootstrap for new providers with no cached baseline, (2) Change detection for providers with existing cached data. This distinction was discovered during design review. Design doc is in progress with Bootstrap Flow section pending.
